FIRST FULL DAY UNDER THE FLAT-STAKE / CONFIDENCE-AS-GAUGE / MED-HIGH-SELECTION REGIME, AND IT CAME UP GREEN. *** 20 model legs, $130 staked, $149.40 returned, NET +$19.40, ROI +14.92%. Wallet trail: $249.16 - $130 + $149.40 = $268.56, ties EXACTLY to live app. THE HEADLINE, AND IT IS A BIG ONE: BOTH BOOKS FINISHED GREEN, AND THE INTERIM READ WAS WRONG. WIN $60 -> $72.00, net +$12.00, ROI +20.0%. PLA $70 -> $77.40, net +$7.40, ROI +10.6%. The interim story (WIN carrying, PLA dead) completely inverted once the later UK races came home. The place pool legs that looked like drag were just unsettled. This is a direct, logged caution against drawing band-conclusions before every leg is in. LESSON 2 - the month-long PLA-lean thesis held up on a full day: PLA cashed 7 places plus contributed to win-class returns, +10.6% ROI, exactly the 'milk the place pool' edge the FLB/parimutuel analysis predicted. But WIN also returned +20% on clean Med-High SA/UK convergence (Black Endeavour, Zinovi, Cruise Control, U S Ranger), so the selection-bias rule (favour Med-High, take WIN where grid+press genuinely converge) is doing real work on BOTH sides. LESSON 3 - the only reds were the pre-flagged ones: AU Tamworth Maxandus (Med-High but short-priced, the tote caveat we called in advance, -$15) and a lone plain-Medium France PLA overlay (Tulsa King -$5). The model's own warnings fired correctly. NET: first full flat-stake day = +14.92% ROI, both books green, 75% strike, reconciled to the cent. Strong validation of the 26/06 regime change, tempered by the honest admission that the interim panic about PLA was premature. Keep taking clean Med-High convergence, keep the faith in the place pool through to full settlement, do not over-react to partial reads.

cols: THE OUTSIDER METHOD — MODEL NOTES
Living document. Update after each day with what worked, what didn't.
CORE PRINCIPLES (v0.1)
• Weight live market odds against form-based picks early — gaps between form favourite and market favourite are signal.
• Discount lightly-raced horses even with strong ratings — small sample size risk.
• Track race-comment language strength (strong vs weak) but weight below stats.
• Score 'value vs odds' as its own factor, separate from 'is this horse good'.
• Track pace/running style — confirmed front-runners bunched together can cook each other.
• Track barrier/draw bias by track over time rather than treating draw as a vague tick-box.
• Separate 'trainer in form' (last 2 weeks strike rate) from 'trainer is reputable'.
• Track confidence calibration — do High confidence picks actually win more than Medium?

• Stake flat until confidence proves predictive — variable staking ($5-$15 by confidence) amplified the wrong picks across days 1-2. Default: flat $10 WIN + $5 PLA, revisit once calibration data justifies sizing up.
MEET TRIAGE PROCESS (added 21/06/2026)

Workflow: user shares the Race 1 card (or just a snippet) for each meet on offer that day, before committing to full racecard pulls for every meet. Claude uses that Race 1 info as a starting point, then searches for the full race card (all races), prize money, feature race status, and any published tipster/pundit analysis for that meet.

Claude grades each meet on: field depth/quality, prize money level, whether it's a feature/Group-Listed day vs a routine fixture, and availability of third-party form analysis to cross-check against. This produces a relative ranking across the meets on offer.

Goal: decide which meet(s) are actually worth applying the Outsider Method to in depth, rather than spreading analysis thin across every available meet. A meet with weak depth, no feature race, and no published analysis is lower priority even if convenient.

MANDATORY EXTERNAL-OPINION CHECK (added 22/06/2026): Whenever the Outsider Method analysis is run on a meet — not just at triage but as a standing part of building the bet slip — Claude actively searches online for tipsters, pundits, press synthesis, and expert opinion on that specific meet/race, and cross-references them against the model picks. The point is not to follow the crowd but to (a) surface anything the model missed, (b) flag where the model diverges from consensus so divergences are deliberate and noted, and (c) capture a press/consensus ranking where one exists (e.g. French Quinte+ press synthesis). If no external opinion can be found for a meet, that is itself recorded against the bets as analysed-but-not-externally-verified, rather than left implied.

Sources to try by region: France/PMU -> Equidia, Geny, Canalturf, Turfoo, Zone-Turf, press synthesis aggregators. UK/IRE -> At The Races, Racing TV, Sporting Life, Timeform. South Africa -> World Sports Betting previews, Raceform, Hollywoodbets. Australia -> Racenet, Punters, racing.com. Hong Kong -> HKJC tips, SCMP. Japan -> netkeiba, FanDuel/model tips. Always note which sources were checked and whether they were found, so the audit trail shows the search was actually done.

This is a pre-screening step only — it does NOT replace the full racecard/tipster PDF/ratings doc review before bets are placed. Once a meet is chosen, the usual full workflow applies: share full racecards, Claude proposes a bet slip, user confirms via SGPools screenshots.

Example precedent: 21/06/2026 meets compared (Malaysia/Selangor, France/Lyon-Parilly, Australia x2, South Africa/Kenilworth, Hong Kong/Sha Tin, Turkey/Istanbul, Korea/Seoul). Hong Kong Sha Tin ranked top on an 11-race card, HK$1.17M Race 1 prize money, live trainers' championship narrative, and full published pundit tips. Selangor ranked second purely for having Last 5 Runs data visible in the racecard itself. Kenilworth ranked lower as a routine fixture with an apprentice maiden as Race 1 (hardest race type to handicap). Lyon-Parilly ranked low as a modest provincial track with no Group race history, a clear step down from Compiegne the day before. Turkey and Korea were not assessed in depth, unfamiliar markets for this model so far.
AUTOMATED TRIAGE TOOL — 'OUTSIDER TRIAGE' WEBAPP (added 22/06/2026)

What it is: a two-stage mobile webapp (hosted on Netlify/Vercel at vibe-arcade-omega.vercel.app/triage2.html, part of hammyLabs) that AUTOMATES the meet-triage step which was previously manual (user screenshotting each meet's Race 1). It scrapes the SingaporePools race-cards page server-side (no CORS), so SGPools remains the single source of truth. Built 22/06/2026.

STAGE 1 (one tap, 'Rank meets'): for the chosen date it pulls every meet's card, scans the WHOLE card for race count + Race-1 field size + class signals, and DETECTS whether SP's analysis PDFs exist (Integrated Form card, iRace Media Selections, Race Analysis). It then scores each meet 0-100 on a transparent 'diveability' weighting: analysis-doc availability (45) + race-count/card depth (25) + class spread, with maiden-heavy cards penalised (20) + R1 field size (10). Meets are ranked, highest score = most worth a deep dive. KEY LESSON BAKED IN: prize money and raw field size are HYGIENE, not edge — the real diveability signal is whether external analysis exists + genuine class spread. This fixed the earlier shallow mistake of ranking a big-field maiden card (e.g. Melbourne) above a doc-rich graded card (e.g. France).

STAGE 2 (per-meet 'Deep read' button): on the 1-2 meets that survive the ranking, the tool fetches and extracts the TEXT of that meet's tipster/analysis PDFs server-side (Media Selections shortlist + Race Analysis summaries/selections). User copies the ranked summary + deep-read text and pastes to Claude. WHERE IT FITS THE BIG PICTURE: this tool REPLACES the manual triage-screenshot step and FEEDS the Mandatory External-Opinion Check above — the SP docs are two of the external sources, but they are both SP-hosted and often echo each other, so Claude must STILL web-search truly independent pundits (Equidia/Geny for France, ratings services for AUS, netkeiba/FanDuel for Japan) to confirm convergence before building the slip. Tool gathers + ranks; the convergence judgement and the full Outsider Method bet-slip build still happen in chat. It does NOT replace the racecard/ratings review before bets are placed.
EXOTIC BETS (added 22/06/2026, trial)

Exotics (forecast, place forecast, tierce/trio, quartet, quinte-style) are allowed as a capped side experiment, NOT a core staking pillar. They ride alongside the flat $10 WIN + $5 PLA core, they do not replace it.

Rules: (1) $5 flat per exotic, same as one unit, never bigger. (2) Only when the model has 3+ genuinely convergent frame horses in one race, the read must be about SHAPE not a single banker. (3) Cap of ONE exotic per meet to start. (4) Logged in Bet Log with Ledger col I = "Exotic" (not "Model"), so they are tracked in their own Running Totals block and never pollute the WIN/PLA split or confidence calibration. (5) BetType col F records the exotic type (e.g. "FORECAST", "TRIO", "QUARTET"). (6) Confidence label optional, exotics are inherently high-variance.  [*** UPDATED 05/07/2026: see dated amendment in Daily Lessons Log — exotic is now CONVERGENCE-GATED (3+ Med-High frame required to trigger), stake corrected from "$5 flat" to $6 = 3x$2 SGPools Trio units (min div $3, 6+ runner races only), and bet-type restricted to order-TOLERANT only (Trio / place-forecast, never straight forecast). Rules (3)-(6) below still stand; the trigger + unit are the amendments. ***]

Hard reminder baked in: right horses in the wrong order still loses (forecast/tierce are order-sensitive). Place-forecast and trio are more forgiving but still harder to hit than a plain place bet. Exotic availability is patchy, e.g. Lyon-Parilly 22/06 offered no place forecast on R1/R4/R5/R8, so exotics cannot be a standard leg on every race.

Review gate: after ~10 settled exotics, check the Exotic ROI and hit rate in Running Totals against the core WIN/PLA ROI. If exotics are not clearly adding value, cut them back or drop them. Treat exactly like the confidence-calibration trial: prove it earns its place before scaling.

RULE CHANGE — EFFECTIVE 26/06/2026
• FLAT-STAKE everything: $10 WIN + $5 PLA on full pairs, $5 on PLA-only picks. No more sizing up or down by confidence.

ROI-BY-CONFIDENCE-BAND ANALYSIS + SELECTION-BIAS RULE (26/06/2026)

INVERTED LADDER CONFIRMED across ~80 legs each side: on BOTH bet types, the High band is among the worst and the middle bands carry the book. 'High' confidence has been actively anti-predictive of ROI. This is the quantified basis for retiring confidence-as-a-staking-input (done 26/06).
*** SELECTION-BIAS RULE - EFFECTIVE 26/06/2026 ***
1. BIAS THE BOOK TOWARD MEDIUM-HIGH CONVERGENCE SPOTS. These are green on PLA (+19.2%) and the least-bad volume band on WIN. Take MORE of them.
2. TRIM marginal Medium and Low-Med PLA insurance legs - the Medium PLA bucket (-20.9%, 34 lines) is the single biggest quiet drag on the place book. Fewer, better place legs, not more of them.

3. This is a SELECTION dial, not a staking dial. Flat-stake rule still holds ($10 WIN + $5 PLA on pairs, $5 PLA-only). 'Bias toward Medium-High' = adjust HOW MANY of each we take, never how much per leg.
4. Confidence label remains a pure prediction/calibration gauge (records what the model expected; moves no money).

5. CAVEAT: Low-Med (5) and (none) (4-5) bands are still thin - do not over-read them. Medium-High (19) and Medium (34) are the trustworthy signals. Monitor band-by-band ROI closely over the coming flat-stake days before hardening anything.
TRACKING: from 26/06, watch Medium-High vs other-band performance day to day. Goal is to confirm the Medium-High edge holds out-of-sample under flat staking before committing further.
STAKING-GATE DISCIPLINE - do NOT size up Medium-High yet (26/06/2026)
Question raised 26/06: since Medium-High PLA is the only green band (+19.2%), should we bump its stake for the next day's bets? Decision: NO. Hold flat. Reasons:

1. SAMPLE TOO THIN: the +19.2% rests on just 19 settled lines. Enough to bias SELECTION toward these spots, not enough to risk more money PER leg. Selection survives a small sample far better than sizing does.

2. DON'T RE-OPEN A CLOSED DOOR: up-sizing by band is variable-staking-by-confidence again, the exact thing retired 26/06. If Medium-High's edge is partly luck, sizing it rebuilds the High-band mistake in a new spot. The flat-stake era deserves a clean run first.

3. PARIMUTUEL WORKS AGAINST IT: SGPools is a tote, so a bigger stake on a popular Medium-High place leg nudges the pool and dilutes your own dividend. Sizing up leans slightly the wrong way on exactly the legs you'd want to press.

4. CAPTURE THE EDGE THE SAFE WAY: taking MORE Medium-High spots at flat stakes captures nearly the same upside as bigger stakes, with a fraction of the downside if the band is noise. Most of the benefit, little of the risk.
THE GATE (prove-it-first, same as confidence-ladder + exotics trials):

- Hold flat ($10 WIN + $5 PLA pairs, $5 PLA-only). Let SELECTION do the work: take every clean Medium-High convergence spot, trim marginal Medium/Low-Med place insurance, let band-count shift toward the edge naturally.
- Only revisit sizing once Medium-High has ~35-40 settled lines AND ROI is still clearly green out-of-sample under flat staking.
- Even then: a GENTLE bump (~$7-8), never a jump to $15, tracked as its own band so the extra dollars can be judged on their own merit.
PRINCIPLE: patience is the edge. The punters who blow up usually don't have bad models, they size up a hot streak that was a small sample. We have a month of honest data - don't spend it early.

EXOTIC RULE AMENDMENT — CONVERGENCE-GATED TRIO (formalised). Exotics may now be PLAYED when the model surfaces a genuine CONVERGENT FRAME, extending the same convergence signal that gates WIN vs PLA on the core book. Trigger + guardrails: (1) TRIGGER = 3+ horses in ONE race that each independently clear the Med-High convergence bar (grid + press + form-panel agreement). The read must be about race SHAPE / who fills the frame, NOT a single banker. One strong horse never triggers an exotic. (2) BET-TYPE = order-TOLERANT only. Trio (first 3 in ANY order) or place-forecast. NEVER order-sensitive types (straight forecast / exacta / tierce-in-order): our signal identifies WHICH horses are live, it has shown NO power to predict finishing ORDER, so the bet must forgive us on order and only test us on frame. (3) STAKE = flat $6 = THREE units of $2 (SGPools Trio unit is $2, min dividend $3, available only on races with 6+ runners — confirmed 05/07 from SGPools bet-info screen). Corrects the earlier loose "$5 flat" note: $5 does not divide into the $2 Trio unit, which is why the app nudged both 23/06 Dieppe + Kenilworth trials from $5 to $6. Cap stays flat, never bigger, never scaled by conviction. (4) CAP = one exotic per meet to start. (5) LEDGER = tag col I "Exotic", BetType col F "TRIO" — kept fully separate so exotics NEVER pollute the WIN/PLA split or confidence calibration. (6) REVIEW GATE unchanged: hold to ~10 settled exotics before any conclusion or scaling; currently only 1 settled (Dieppe TRIO, Lose -$6) + 1 refund, so this remains a forward-looking trial with NO data endorsement yet.

EXOTIC RULE — RATIONALE + HONEST CAVEAT (banked with the amendment above). WHY it fits: the core method reads race SHAPE (which horses hit the frame) better than the single winner — a Trio expresses exactly that, so gating it on a 3+ convergent frame is a disciplined extension of the existing signal, not a new punt. WHY order-tolerant only: a month of data shows the signal finds competitive horses but says nothing about ordering; forecasts/exactas demand the one thing we cannot do. HONEST CAVEAT: exotic maths is unforgiving — pools carry a bigger takeout than WIN/PLA, the combinatorial ordering space makes the true odds steep, and even a good frame-selector can lose steadily if order is effectively random. Behavioural note (favourite-longshot cousin): exotic pools exist BECAUSE punters overvalue small-stake/huge-payout dreams (the same crowd bias that inverted our confidence bands), and tracks historically pushed trifectas/exotics precisely for their fatter takeout. So we wade into the most treacherous pool deliberately, capped and logged. FRAMING: treat as a small capped side-experiment, not a profit centre — if it works we found something rare; if not, the flat $6 cap + separate ledger mean it cost almost nothing to learn.
